D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

Mach Bands

slide the contrast across a ramp and watch bright and dark bands appear at edges that aren't really there

A centre-surround cell reports the difference between a point and its neighbourhood, so it overshoots where a ramp turns into a plateau — inventing a band of light or dark.

Look at the grey strip: a faint bright band hugs the top of the ramp and a dark band hugs the bottom. Slide the contrast and the surround width, and compare the true luminance with the perceived curve below.
